name: Lemon Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a 4-inch band of triple-thickness aluminum foil that is 2 inches longer than the circumference of a 6-cup souffle dish. Extend the dish by securing the band around the top edge. Alternatively, use a 2-quart round casserole dish.
2. Mix 3/4 cup sugar, the water, lemon juice, salt, and gelatin in a saucepan.
3. Beat egg yolks slightly; stir into the gelatin mixture. Heat just to boiling over medium heat, stirring constantly, then remove from heat. Stir in lemon peel.
4. Place the pan in a bowl of ice and water, or refrigerate for 20 to 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture mounds slightly when dropped from a spoon. If the mixture becomes too thick, place the pan in a bowl of hot water and stir constantly until it reaches the proper consistency.
5. Beat egg whites in a large bowl until foamy. Add sugar gradually, beating continuously until stiff and glossy. Do not underbeat.
6. Fold the gelatin mixture into the egg whites.
7. Beat whipping cream in a chilled medium bowl until stiff. Gently fold the whipped cream into the egg white mixture.
8. Carefully turn the batter into the souffle dish. Refrigerate about 8 hours or until set.
9. Remove the foil band before serving. Any remaining souffle can be refrigerated.
